subject: First Aid & Safety
title: Electrical Safety Principles
description: A 60-second lesson on Electrical Safety Principles.

hook.text: It takes only 100 milliamps to kill you. A Christmas tree light draws 10 times that.

buildup.text: Electricity follows the path of least resistance—and your body is a great conductor.

discovery.text: Current, not voltage, kills. Wet conditions lower skin resistance, increasing lethal risk.

twist.text: Most electrical deaths happen at common household voltage—110V is enough if conditions are right.

climax.text: GFCIs detect current leaks and cut power in 1/40th of a second—preventing electrocution.

punchline.text: 110 volts. Wet hands. That's all it takes. Respect electricity.

quiz.question: What primarily determines whether an electrical shock is lethal?
options: The amount of current flowing through the body
